title: plectranthus purpuratus subsp. montanus
intro: Plectranthus purpuratus subsp. montanus, also known as Mountain Spurflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of South Africa and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as to alleviate symptoms of menopause and digestive issues, thanks to its hormonal balancing and anti-inflammatory properties. Medicinal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
